file_insights.purpose: Level 4 TrueColor terminal UI renderer with 24-bit RGB support
key_components: state_rgb() - Maps card states (pending/running/done/failed/merged) to RGB tuples
lerp_u8() - Linear interpolation for smooth color gradients
gradient_fg() - Per-cell RGB escape for progress bar (amber #B8690F → green #1E8A45)
dice_glyph()/moon_glyph() - Priority and phase progress indicators
col_width() - Adapts layout width based on single vs two-column mode
TrueColorRenderer trait impl - Renders section headers, card rows, progress bars, footers
file_insights.test_coverage: 28 unit tests covering headers, card rows, progress bars, two-column layout, and RGB escape sequences

patterns_discovered.description: 24-bit RGB colors use \x1b[38;2;R;G;Bm format with per-cell interpolation
patterns_discovered.implementation: state_rgb() returns formatted string; gradient_fg() interpolates between amber and green

patterns_discovered.description: Double-line outer boxes (╔═╗║╚╝) contain rounded single-line card separators (╭─╮│╰╯)
patterns_discovered.implementation: Section headers use double-line; cards use rounded separators for visual nesting

patterns_discovered.description: Inner width accounts for box frame (5 chars: '║ ╭' prefix + '╮' suffix)
patterns_discovered.implementation: col_width() halves terminal width in two-column mode; inner_width() subtracts 5 for box frame

patterns_discovered.description: Each card state has a visual marker (▶ running, ✓ done/merged, ✗ failed, · default)
patterns_discovered.implementation: render_card_row() uses match on card.state to select marker

patterns_discovered.description: Progress bar cells are individually colored using interpolated RGB from start to end
patterns_discovered.implementation: Each filled cell at index i calculates t = i/(bar_width-1), applies gradient_fg(t)

patterns_discovered.description: Dice glyphs (⚀-⚅) for priority 1-6; moon phases (◔◑◕●) for progress fractions
patterns_discovered.implementation: dice_glyph() matches priority; moon_glyph() uses threshold-based selection

gotchas_discovered.detail: saturating_sub() prevents panic when box frame exceeds terminal width on very narrow terminals
gotchas_discovered.impact: Critical for robustness on terminals < 50 chars wide

gotchas_discovered.detail: When bar_width <= 1, t is forced to 0.0 to prevent division by zero
gotchas_discovered.impact: Prevents NaN in interpolation; ensures stable gradient coloring even on tiny progress bars

gotchas_discovered.detail: RESET must follow each color change to prevent color bleed to following text
gotchas_discovered.impact: Missing reset causes all subsequent output to inherit previous color

gotchas_discovered.detail: col_width() divides terminal width by 2; requires both-column layout consideration
gotchas_discovered.impact: Single-column at width < 100; two-column at >= 100 per spec

gotchas_discovered.detail: All characters must be U+0000-U+FFFF; no astral plane characters
gotchas_discovered.impact: Restricts emoji/special characters; maintains compatibility with narrow UTF-8 handling

gotchas_discovered.detail: state_rgb() uses exact RGB tuples matching colors.rs for consistency
gotchas_discovered.impact: Mismatched RGB values break visual state identification

features_implemented: Section headers with state-colored double-line boxes
Card rows with state markers, priority dice, provider, elapsed time, progress, exit code
Progress bars with per-cell RGB gradient from amber to green
Footer with statistics (total, success rate, avg duration)
Rounded card separators within double-line boxes
approach_outcome.test_results: 28 unit tests passing, covering RGB escapes, box drawing, layout adaptation, state coloring, and two-column logic
approach_outcome.commit: 90d6a08 auto-claude: subtask-3-3 - Implement TrueColorRenderer in render/truecolor.rs

recommendations.suggestion: Verify state_rgb() tuples match colors.rs state_color() values to ensure visual consistency across renderer implementations
Consider rounding t = i / (bar_width - 1) to avoid floating-point jitter in gradient transitions
Add runtime detection for terminal width < 40 to gracefully degrade layout (single-char boxes or ASCII fallback)
Add tests for width=39 (below two-column threshold) and width=8 (sub-minimal) to verify saturation behavior
If render_card_row() is called in tight loops, consider caching color escape sequences in a lookup table
Document the exact RGB hex values (e.g., '#B8690F' amber) in comments for cross-reference with design specs
